tossups.question: At age 14, Stephen Shore cold-called this photographer and sold three photos to begin his career. This avid horticulturist took the photo Heavy Roses and exhibited many new breeds of delphinium. This photographer likened an actress to a leopardess behind shrubbery in a photo of her face with a piece of black lace suspended in front of it. This photographer won an Oscar for directing the World War II documentary The (*) Fighting Lady. Inspired by both Whistler’s Nocturnes and an earlier photo of the same subject, this photographer used gum bichromate in his image of the Flatiron Building. This native Luxembourger shot The Pond—Moonlight and chose 503 photos from 68 countries for a 1955 event at the MoMA. For 10 points, Alfred Stieglitz co-founded the Photo-Secessionist gallery 291 with what curator of the exhibition The Family of Man?
tossups.question_sanitized: At age 14, Stephen Shore cold-called this photographer and sold three photos to begin his career. This avid horticulturist took the photo Heavy Roses and exhibited many new breeds of delphinium. This photographer likened an actress to a leopardess behind shrubbery in a photo of her face with a piece of black lace suspended in front of it. This photographer won an Oscar for directing the World War II documentary The (*) Fighting Lady. Inspired by both Whistler's Nocturnes and an earlier photo of the same subject, this photographer used gum bichromate in his image of the Flatiron Building. This native Luxembourger shot The Pond-Moonlight and chose 503 photos from 68 countries for a 1955 event at the MoMA. For 10 points, Alfred Stieglitz co-founded the Photo-Secessionist gallery 291 with what curator of the exhibition The Family of Man?
tossups.answer: Edward Steichen [or Éduard Jean Steichen] (The actress in the third sentence is Gloria Swanson.)
tossups.answer_sanitized: Edward Steichen [or Eduard Jean Steichen] (The actress in the third sentence is Gloria Swanson.)

tossups.question: It’s not Timothy, but an artist with this first name worked with Alexander Upshaw on the western images Canyon De Chelly and Old Eagle. Another artist with this first name depicted a nude Charis Wilson sitting on a blanket in Santa Monica. An artist with this first name was funded by J. P. Morgan to create the photobook The North American Indian and had the surname Curtis. Another artist with this first name pioneered adding color with layers of (*) gum bichromate print, and curated the MoMA exhibition The Family of Man. This is the first name of a Pictorialist who photographed the “still-lifes” Nautilus and Pepper No. 30, as well as another who created The Pond-Moonlight and The Flatiron. For 10 points, give this first name of photographers surnamed Weston and Steichen.
tossups.question_sanitized: It's not Timothy, but an artist with this first name worked with Alexander Upshaw on the western images Canyon De Chelly and Old Eagle. Another artist with this first name depicted a nude Charis Wilson sitting on a blanket in Santa Monica. An artist with this first name was funded by J. P. Morgan to create the photobook The North American Indian and had the surname Curtis. Another artist with this first name pioneered adding color with layers of (*) gum bichromate print, and curated the MoMA exhibition The Family of Man. This is the first name of a Pictorialist who photographed the "still-lifes" Nautilus and Pepper No. 30, as well as another who created The Pond-Moonlight and The Flatiron. For 10 points, give this first name of photographers surnamed Weston and Steichen.
tossups.answer: Edward [accept Edward Steichen or Edward Jean Steichen; accept Edward Weston or Edward Henry Weston; accept Edward S. Curtis or Edward Sheriff Curtis]
tossups.answer_sanitized: Edward [accept Edward Steichen or Edward Jean Steichen; accept Edward Weston or Edward Henry Weston; accept Edward S. Curtis or Edward Sheriff Curtis]

bonuses.leadin: With Alfred Stieglitz, this photographer co-founded the Little Galleries of the Photo-Secession, later known as Gallery 291. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this photographer who captured the Flatiron Building at night behind some tree branches and curated the exhibit The Family of Man.

Steichen used gum bichromate to color The Pond–Moonlight, exemplifying this style popular among Photo-Secessionists that, unlike straight photography, involves manipulating photos for aesthetic effect.
Steichen also pioneered the use of photography for magazines in this industry. Steichen was chief photographer for Vogue and Vanity Fair, which focus on this industry.
answers_sanitized: fashion industry [or clothing industry; or apparel industry]
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: With Alfred Stieglitz, this photographer co-founded the Little Galleries of the Photo-Secession, later known as Gallery 291. For 10 points each:
parts_sanitized: Name this photographer who captured the Flatiron Building at night behind some tree branches and curated the exhibit The Family of Man.

Steichen also pioneered the use of photography for magazines in this industry. Steichen was chief photographer for Vogue and Vanity Fair, which focus on this industry.

bonuses.leadin: This artist, whose 1911 pictures of Paul Poiret's gowns for Art et Decoration magazine were the first modern fashion photographs, was chief photographer for Vogue and Vanity Fair in much of the 20s and 30s. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this curator of the MoMA's The Family of Man exhibit, whose commercial turn was bemoaned by fellow Photo-Secessionist Alfred Stieglitz.

Steichen's photograph of Gloria Swanson behind a veil does not show this trademark facial feature of hers. A celebrated Vogue cover only shows Jean Patchett's eye, lips, and one of these moles whose two-word name reflects their attractive status.
Swanson was a client of this Polish-Jewish popularizer of the noun "make-up," who used a "beauty micrometer" to analyze faces. His namesake company moved a drawn "X" from inside its logo to the end.

bonuses.leadin: Answer the following about artists serving as subjects for photographers. For 10 points each:
parts: Splashing water in the shape of a waterfall obscures the title artist who can be seen floating next to three black cats and one of his own paintings in “Dali Atomicus”, a work by this Lithuanian-American photographer.

This American photographer used a sculpture by his friend Auguste Rodin designed as a monument to Balzac for his photograph Towards the Light, Midnight. This artist co-founded the Photo-Secessionist movement with Alfred Stieglitz and organized the influential Family of Man exhibit in 1955.
While visiting his villa at La Rêve, Henri Cartier-Bresson photographed this other artist of Harmony in Red and The Dance as he sat amongst his pet birds in his studio.
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: Answer the following about artists serving as subjects for photographers. For 10 points each:
parts_sanitized: Splashing water in the shape of a waterfall obscures the title artist who can be seen floating next to three black cats and one of his own paintings in "Dali Atomicus", a work by this Lithuanian-American photographer.

This American photographer used a sculpture by his friend Auguste Rodin designed as a monument to Balzac for his photograph Towards the Light, Midnight. This artist co-founded the Photo-Secessionist movement with Alfred Stieglitz and organized the influential Family of Man exhibit in 1955.
While visiting his villa at La Reve, Henri Cartier-Bresson photographed this other artist of Harmony in Red and The Dance as he sat amongst his pet birds in his studio.

bonuses.leadin: Narrator Robert Taylor informs the viewers of one of this man’s films that all the servicemen shown have been killed, in The Fighting Lady. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this photographer of The Pond—Moonlight who co-founded the Photo Secession movement with Alfred Stieglitz.

Edward Steichen photographed a person of this profession with “La Serpentine” in 1909. Another Steichen photograph depicts the dark profile of a man of this profession sitting pensively in its right foreground.

Steichen was the head of the Department of Photography at this city’s Museum of Modern Art, where he curated his exhibit The Family of Man. Diane Arbus photographed a child with a toy hand grenade in a park in this city.
answers: artists [or sculptors or painters; prompt on Henri Matisse by asking “what profession?”] (The second photograph is Rodin with His Sculptures “Victor Hugo” and “The Thinker”.)
answers_sanitized: artists [or sculptors or painters; prompt on Henri Matisse by asking "what profession?"] (The second photograph is Rodin with His Sculptures "Victor Hugo" and "The Thinker".)

parts_sanitized: Name this photographer of The Pond-Moonlight who co-founded the Photo Secession movement with Alfred Stieglitz.

Steichen was the head of the Department of Photography at this city's Museum of Modern Art, where he curated his exhibit The Family of Man. Diane Arbus photographed a child with a toy hand grenade in a park in this city.

bonuses.leadin: This man photographed Marcel Duchamp’s Fountain using a Marsden Hartley painting as a background. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this husband of Georgia O’Keefe, who photographed working-class passengers boarding a ship in The Steerage

Like Stieglitz, this photographer photographed the Flatiron Building, but produced a blue background in his photograph using a gum bichromate solution. He curated the first Family of Man exhibit at the MoMA.

This man used a special camera that shot ninety degrees off from the opposite direction in which it was pointed to take candid photographs such as one of a beggar holding a sign saying “Blind”. His photograph Wall Street shows people dwarfed by the dark rectangular niches of the J.P. Morgan building.

bonuses.leadin: His works infuenced by Swedenborg include a picture of a bountiful farm titled Peace and Plenty and his three-part series The Triumph of the Cross. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this American artist, a leader of the Tonalist school of neutral-colored atmospheric landscape painting, who did many landscapes of Montclair New Jersey at the end of his life including The Home of the Heron.
George Inness led one branch of American tonalism, while the other branch was informally led by this American expatri-ate who sued John Ruskin in a famous libel trial and painted several “nocturnes”.

The neutral palette of Tonalist painting later infuenced this photographer whose The Pond - Moonlight fetched the highest price for any photograph at auction. He also designed the cover art for the journal Camera Work.

bonuses.leadin: This photographer’s The Pond – Moonlight, which had to be manually colorized with gums, once held the title of most expensive photograph ever. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this American photographer who created the MoMA exhibit The Family of Man and helped create the gallery 291 with Alfred Stieglitz.
Steichen’s The Family of Man exhibit featured this photograph by Dorothea Lange of Florence Owens Thompson during the Great Depression.
Edward Steichen was the most oft-featured artist in this quarterly journal of fine art photography that was founded and published by Alfred Stieglitz.

bonuses.leadin: This photographer's early work included pictures of gowns designed by the couturier Paul Poiret. For 10 points each:
parts: Identify this American photographer who curated the Family of Man exhibition at the MoMA in 1955 and assisted Alfred Stieglitz in setting up the 291 Gallery, which introduced works by Matisse, Picasso, and others to the United States.
Steichen's work was often printed in this quarterly photographic journal published by Alfred Stieglitz.

Steichen is regarded as a pioneer in this photography genre because of his work for the Conde Nast Publications Vogue and Vanity Fair. Another photographer in this genre, Richard Avedon, created several advertisements for Christian Dior.

bonuses.leadin: An early color photo taken by this man at Mamaroneck, New York became what was then the most expensive photograph ever sold. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this artist of The Pond - Moonlight, a major Pictorialist photographer whose other works include The Flatiron. He also took war photography during both World Wars and helped curate the 291 gallery.

This Pictorialist artist of Spring Showers, the Coach, and Winter, Fifth Avenue also organized the 291 art gallery. He may be better known for The Steerage and many portraits of his wife Georgia O’Keeffe.
After serving as editor for Camera Notes, Stieglitz began publishing this photography journal, which featured artists like Steichen and Paul Strand. The Steerage was first featured in this journal.

bonuses.leadin: It became the world’s most expensive photograph when it was sold for 2.9 million in 2006. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this pictorialist photograph, which was taken at a location in Mamaroneck, New York at a certain time of day. Its photographer manually layered light-sensitive gums over the negative to create the impression of color.

The Pond-Moonlight was created by this photographer, who pioneered fashion photography for Vogue and Vanity Fair in the 1920s and 30s. He also organized The Family of Man exhibition at the Modern Museum of Art.

This modern photographer depicted a naked John Lennon lying in bed with a clothed Yoko Ono during her time as the chief photographer for Rolling Stone. Her many pictures of celebrities include one of Demi Moore holding her pregnant stomach for Vanity Fair.
